meta.source_paper: arxiv 2308.01703 (ACM Web Conference 2024), Tornado Cash deposit analysis (Chainalysis), Privacy Pools (Buterin et al. 2023)

risk_domain.name: Post-Mixer Linkability
risk_domain.description: Behavioral heuristics that link deposits to withdrawals across mixing protocols (Tornado Cash, Railgun, Privacy Pools) despite correct cryptographic privacy. Generalizes stealth_address_ops H3 (timing) and H6 (amounts) to the broader mixing context, adding multi-denomination, cross-protocol, and mixer-specific metadata leaks.

capabilities: full on-chain observation of all deposits and withdrawals across all mixing protocols
timing analysis across mempool and block timestamps with second-level precision
amount distribution modeling across fixed and variable denomination pools
cross-protocol behavioral correlation (mixer withdrawal -> DeFi interaction)
relay and gas pattern analysis for mixer-specific transaction fingerprinting
limitations: no ZK proof internals (cannot break cryptographic privacy directly)
no off-chain metadata (IP, browser) unless combined with RPC provider profile

H1_timing_correlation.name: Timing correlation
H1_timing_correlation.description: Deposit-to-withdrawal timing narrows the anonymity set. If a withdrawal occurs shortly after a deposit of the same denomination, the set of possible depositors shrinks to those who deposited within that window. With few concurrent deposits, timing alone can be near-deterministic.

signals.name: short_dwell_time
signals.description: Time between deposit and withdrawal is less than 1 hour in a pool with fewer than 50 concurrent deposits

signals.name: medium_dwell_time
signals.description: Dwell time is less than 6 hours with fewer than 200 concurrent deposits in the pool

signals.name: consistent_dwell_pattern
signals.description: User consistently withdraws within a similar time window across multiple mixing operations, creating a behavioral fingerprint

detection.threshold: short_dwell_time triggers BLOCK, consistent_dwell_pattern triggers WARNING

recommendations.description: Wait a random period (12-72 hours) before withdrawing. Longer delays for smaller pools.
Time withdrawal to coincide with highest deposit activity in the pool to maximize anonymity set

H1_timing_correlation.fundamental_limitation: With few mixer users, even long delays provide small anonymity sets. If the pool has 10 depositors per day, a 24-hour delay still narrows to ~10 candidates. Timing mitigation requires more users.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Simulate deposit/withdrawal streams at varying activity levels (10, 100, 1000 deposits/day). Adversary matches by timing windows.
benchmark_scenario.metric: adversary accuracy in linking deposit-withdrawal pairs at each activity level
benchmark_scenario.baseline: Tornado Cash 0.1 ETH pool: median anonymity set ~20 (low activity periods much worse)

H2_amount_fingerprinting.name: Amount fingerprinting
H2_amount_fingerprinting.description: Unique or unusual amounts link deposits to withdrawals. Fixed-denomination pools (Tornado Cash) mitigate this partially, but variable-amount protocols (Railgun, Privacy Pools) expose the full amount vector. Even in fixed pools, the total amount deposited across denominations creates a fingerprint.

signals.name: unique_amount_in_pool
signals.description: In variable-amount pools, the deposit amount has fewer than 3 matches within a 24-hour window

signals.name: precise_non_round_amount
signals.description: Amount has more than 4 decimal places, making it statistically unique even in active pools

signals.name: total_amount_fingerprint
signals.description: Total value deposited across multiple fixed-denomination deposits forms a unique combination (e.g., 3x 1ETH + 2x 0.1ETH = 3.2ETH target)

detection.threshold: unique_amount_in_pool triggers BLOCK, total_amount_fingerprint triggers WARNING

recommendations.description: Prefer fixed-denomination pools (Tornado Cash style) over variable-amount shielded pools for high-privacy transactions
Round to the nearest standard denomination and leave dust in source wallet

H2_amount_fingerprinting.fundamental_limitation: Variable-amount pools fundamentally leak more information than fixed-denomination pools. The trade-off is UX (exact amounts) vs privacy (denomination buckets). Privacy Pools may mitigate via association sets, but the amount itself is still a signal.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Pool of N deposits with realistic amount distribution. Adversary matches by amount + timing combination.
benchmark_scenario.metric: adversary's deposit-to-withdrawal linking accuracy for fixed vs variable denomination pools
benchmark_scenario.baseline: unique amounts in variable pools: ~95% linkable; fixed pools: timing-only attack

H3_multi_denomination_linkage.name: Multi-denomination linkage
H3_multi_denomination_linkage.description: Splitting a target amount across multiple denomination pools (e.g., 3.2 ETH as 3x 1ETH + 2x 0.1ETH) creates a multi-deposit fingerprint. The combination of denominations, deposit timing pattern, and withdrawal timing pattern narrows the anonymity set to users who made the same set of deposits.

signals.name: correlated_multi_pool_deposits
signals.description: Multiple deposits to different denomination pools from addresses in the same cluster within a short time window

signals.name: matching_withdrawal_pattern
signals.description: Withdrawals from multiple pools to addresses in the same cluster with timing that matches the deposit pattern

signals.name: denomination_sum_fingerprint
signals.description: The sum of denominations deposited matches the sum withdrawn within a reasonable time window, creating a value fingerprint

recommendations.description: Spread multi-denomination deposits across days or weeks from different addresses to break temporal correlation
Use only one denomination pool and accept dust loss to avoid multi-pool fingerprinting
recommendations.user_cost: medium (dust loss, may need many transactions)

H3_multi_denomination_linkage.fundamental_limitation: Multi-denomination usage is inherent when transferring non-round amounts through fixed-denomination pools. The only full mitigation is variable-amount pools (which have their own fingerprinting problem -- H2).

benchmark_scenario.setup: Simulate N users each splitting amounts across 2-4 denomination pools. Adversary correlates deposit and withdrawal patterns.
benchmark_scenario.metric: fraction of users linkable via multi-denomination deposit/withdrawal pattern analysis
benchmark_scenario.baseline: Tornado Cash: significant fraction of users deposited to multiple pools within hours

H4_cross_protocol_linkage.name: Cross-protocol linkage
H4_cross_protocol_linkage.description: Mixer withdrawal immediately used in DeFi (swap, lend, bridge) links the withdrawal to a behavioral identity. The adversary does not need to break the mixer -- they just observe what happens after the withdrawal. A unique DeFi interaction pattern after withdrawal can narrow the anonymity set to one.

signals.name: immediate_defi_interaction
signals.description: Withdrawal address interacts with a DeFi protocol within 2 blocks of receiving mixer funds

signals.name: behavioral_continuity
signals.description: Post-withdrawal DeFi interaction pattern matches a known address cluster's historical behavior (same protocols, similar amounts, same time-of-day)

signals.name: bridge_after_mixer
signals.description: Funds bridged to another chain within 1 hour of mixer withdrawal, creating cross-chain correlation opportunity

detection.threshold: immediate_defi_interaction triggers WARNING, behavioral_continuity triggers BLOCK

recommendations.description: Wait at least 24 hours and multiple intervening transactions before using mixed funds in identifiable DeFi protocols
Vary the DeFi protocols, amounts, and timing used after mixer withdrawal to avoid matching historical behavioral fingerprint

H4_cross_protocol_linkage.fundamental_limitation: Post-mixer behavior is outside the mixer's control. The mixer provides unlinkability at the deposit/withdrawal boundary only. Everything the user does after withdrawal is in the clear and can be correlated.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Track N mixer withdrawals and their subsequent on-chain activity within 24 hours. Adversary attempts to match withdrawals to known behavioral profiles.
benchmark_scenario.metric: fraction of withdrawals linkable to an identity via post-withdrawal DeFi behavior
benchmark_scenario.baseline: Chainalysis reports linking Tornado Cash withdrawals via post-withdrawal behavior in multiple investigations

H5_mixer_specific_metadata.name: Mixer-specific metadata leaks
H5_mixer_specific_metadata.description: Gas patterns, relay usage, withdrawal address reuse, and protocol-specific metadata fingerprint mixer users. Relayer choice narrows the set (few relayers = few anonymity groups). Withdrawal address reuse across multiple mixing operations links them. Non-standard gas settings identify specific wallets.

signals.name: relayer_fingerprint
signals.description: Withdrawal uses a specific relayer that handles <5% of pool withdrawals, narrowing anonymity set

signals.name: withdrawal_address_reuse
signals.description: Same address used as withdrawal destination for multiple separate mixing operations

signals.name: gas_pattern_fingerprint
signals.description: Withdrawal transaction gas price or priority fee is distinctive (>2 std devs from block median or matches a known wallet configuration)

detection.threshold: withdrawal_address_reuse triggers BLOCK, relayer_fingerprint triggers WARNING

recommendations.description: Generate a new address for every mixer withdrawal. Never reuse withdrawal addresses across mixing operations.
Choose the relayer with the highest market share to maximize the anonymity set of relayer users
Use the relayer's default gas parameters rather than custom settings to blend with other relayer users

H5_mixer_specific_metadata.fundamental_limitation: Mixer metadata leakage is protocol-design-dependent. Each mixer has different relayer architectures, gas handling, and withdrawal mechanisms. Countermeasures must be adapted per protocol.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Analyze N Tornado Cash withdrawals: classify by relayer, gas pattern, and address reuse. Adversary clusters withdrawals by metadata.
benchmark_scenario.metric: fraction of withdrawals distinguishable from the anonymity set via metadata alone (without timing or amount analysis)
benchmark_scenario.baseline: Tornado Cash: top 3 relayers handled >80% of withdrawals; address reuse was common in early usage

timing_delay.description: Queue mixer withdrawal for delayed submission after random interval, calibrated to pool activity level

amount_normalizer.description: Round deposit amount to nearest standard denomination for the target mixer protocol

pool_monitor.description: Monitor deposit/withdrawal activity across mixer pools to recommend optimal timing, denomination, and relayer for maximum anonymity set

combined_benchmark.description: End-to-end benchmark measuring post-mixer linkability with all behavioral heuristics applied

methodology.step_1: Generate synthetic deposit/withdrawal dataset with realistic timing, amount, and behavioral distributions across multiple mixer protocols
methodology.step_2: Run adversary simulator applying all 5 heuristics (timing, amount, multi-denomination, cross-protocol, metadata)
methodology.step_3: Measure baseline linking rate per heuristic and combined
methodology.step_4: Apply profile recommendations to all operations
methodology.step_5: Re-run adversary simulator on modified operations
methodology.step_6: Compare: linking rate before vs after, broken down by pool activity level

combined_benchmark.target: Reduce combined linking rate from ~30-50% (estimated baseline) to <5% with all recommendations applied

adversary_levels.name: advanced
adversary_levels.description: All heuristics including cross-protocol behavioral correlation

templates.risk_assessment: Mixer operation triggers {heuristic_id} ({heuristic_name}): {signal_description}. Confidence: {confidence}. Anonymity set: ~{anon_set}. Recommendation: {recommendation}.
templates.summary: Analyzed {n_txs} mixer operations. {n_risky} linkability risks detected ({pct_risky}%). Average anonymity set: ~{avg_anon_set}. Top risks: {top_risks}.
templates.summary_clean: All {n_txs} mixer operations pass linkability checks. Anonymity sets adequate.
templates.skill_suggestion: To mitigate {heuristic_name}, I can {skill_action}. This improves anonymity set by ~{effectiveness_pct}%. {user_cost_note}
templates.pool_recommendation: Pool {pool_name}: {n_deposits} deposits in last {window}h. Optimal withdrawal time: {peak_hour}:00 UTC ({peak_count} withdrawals/hr). Anonymity set: ~{anon_set}.
templates.cross_protocol_warning: WARNING: Immediate DeFi interaction after mixer withdrawal. {defi_protocol} interaction within {minutes} minutes links this withdrawal to behavioral profile. Recommendation: wait {recommended_delay}h.
